verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 is a trans-Golgi network (TGN) resident protein with no documented surface-accessible domain. The NCBI summary explicitly states it is 'localized to the trans-Golgi network' and cycles between early endosomes and the TGN—classic endomembrane compartments. Type I integral membrane proteins of the TGN remain intracellular; their N-terminal luminal domain faces the Golgi lumen (topologically equivalent to the ER lumen), not the extracellular space. No documented surface trafficking, plasma membrane localization, shedding ectodomain, or dual localization to the PM is mentioned. No therapeutic programs target TGOLN2 on the cell surface. The protein lacks the mechanisms needed to reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane.
